objective: Prove current-host viability and lock the strict contracts, official Firecracker release, installed Codex automation seam, dependency set, synthetic scenario, and host-owned model proxy boundary.
assumptions: The Operator-supplied current build map authorizes Stage 2 implementation despite the earlier planning-only workspace handoff text.
Host implementation uses Python 3.14 standard library only; no third-party Python dependency class is admitted.
The guest receives a short-lived CRUMPLE_RUN_CAPABILITY only; it never receives provider authentication.
The host live provider reads CRUMPLE_OPENAI_API_KEY only at call time from an Operator-controlled runtime environment or secret store.
The official Firecracker v1.16.1 x86-64 archive is the pinned VMM artifact for this target.
Hashes establish identity and integrity, not safety.

commands_executed.command: uname -srm; inspect /dev/kvm; inspect virtualization flags and KVM modules
commands_executed.concise_output: Linux x86-64; virtualization CPU flag present; kvm and kvm_amd loaded; unrestricted /dev/kvm readable and writable.

commands_executed.command: codex --version; codex --help; codex exec --help; inspect official Codex custom-provider and exec documentation
commands_executed.concise_output: codex-cli 0.144.4; exec supports ephemeral JSONL automation and strict config; custom providers support Responses wire API and bearer-token seams.

commands_executed.command: download official Firecracker v1.16.1 x86_64 archive and published checksum; sha256sum --check; internal SHA256SUMS verification; firecracker --version; jailer --version
commands_executed.concise_output: Archive checksum OK; all internal artifacts OK; Firecracker and Jailer v1.16.1.

commands_executed.command: PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m unittest discover -s tests -v
commands_executed.concise_output: 15 tests passed after one preserved bounded repair.

commands_executed.command: git diff --check; git status --short; SHA-256 scenario/tool surface; credential-literal scan
commands_executed.concise_output: No whitespace errors, only declared untracked Phase 0 paths, scenario hashes match locks, no API-key-shaped literal found.

tests_and_falsifiers.test: All eight JSON schema documents parse and positive fixtures validate
Unknown run-request fields including host paths are rejected
Out-of-enum authority and semantic authority promotion are rejected
Every hostile fixture string is absent from and cannot be inserted into the trusted projection limitation vocabulary
Guest-controlled semantic fields are rejected by trusted_projection.v1
Mock proxy enforces capability, endpoint, model, request count, request size, response size, and TTL
Arbitrary custom-provider destination is rejected by guest-codex.v1 invocation seam
Installed Codex executes ephemerally against mock Responses provider using only run capability
Live provider without Operator credential fails closed

limitations_and_checks_not_run: No reusable or placeholder provider credential was created, copied, stored, or used.
A Firecracker microVM was not booted in Phase 0; lifecycle execution belongs to Phase 1.
The Codex seam proof ran as a host process against loopback; guest transport is exercised in Phase 2.
No claim is made about general host observation of guest syscalls, filesystem reads, or hidden model state.
detected_drift: Initial restricted execution context hid /dev/kvm although host KVM modules and CPU virtualization were present; the Operator changed the execution environment, after which /dev/kvm was directly verified. No product or trust-boundary change resulted.

repair_attempts.original_failure: Installed Codex seam test exited 2 because codex exec 0.144.4 rejected --ask-for-approval in subcommand position.
repair_attempts.allowed_scope: tests/test_codex_proxy_seam.py only; preserve provider, auth, and trust boundaries.
repair_attempts.repair: Replaced the unsupported flag with strict config override approval_policy="never".
repair_attempts.retest_output: 2 seam tests passed after the configuration-seam extraction.

repair_attempts.original_failure: The completed Phase 0 receipt failed its own schema because the WORKTREE_UNCOMMITTED sentinel used uppercase letters outside A-F.
repair_attempts.allowed_scope: contracts/phase_receipt.schema.json and append-only repair history in receipts/phase-0.json.
repair_attempts.repair: Widened only the WORKTREE_ sentinel suffix to uppercase A-Z, digits, and underscore; committed SHA matching remains exactly 40 lowercase hexadecimal characters.
